title: Nerium undulatum
intro: Nerium undulatum, also known as California oleander, is a flowering plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Nerium undulatum include oleandrin, neriine, and neriidin, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are tinctures and extracts, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, including arthritis, wounds, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Nerium undulatum is classified within the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as dogbane or oleander family. This classification places Nerium undulatum within the order Gentianales, along with other flowering plants such as gentians and willows. The genus Nerium is further divided into a few species, with Nerium undulatum being one of them. Nerium undulatum is a species of flowering plant native to Western Australia. Within the classification, Nerium undulatum is also known by its common name, Blue Bottlebrush.
uses: Nerium undulatum, also known as California fuchsia, has been used in traditional medicine by various Native American tribes. The plant's roots and leaves have been employed to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. In addition, the plant's roots have been used to treat digestive issues and as a diuretic. More research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential uses.

constituents: Nerium undulatum, also known as California oleander, contains several active constituents, including cardenolides, which are a group of cardiac glycosides. These compounds are responsible for the plant's toxic effects and have been studied for their potential medicinal properties. The cardenolides in Nerium undulatum can cause cardiac arrhythmias, nausea, and vomiting, highlighting the plant's toxicity. Additionally, the plant contains other bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. Further research is needed to fully understand the active constituents and potential applications of Nerium undulatum.
parts: Nerium undulatum, also known as the California fuchsia, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits.
